element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Practiced throughout early levels against basic monster encounters
Found scattered; dealt more damage than unarmed strikes
Collected to fill meter; enables transformation at full capacity
Rick becomes stronger, executes special chest-extension attack; flings weapons for greater damage
Players navigate mansion rooms with provided map; forced to backtrack to find exits
Timing affects plot outcomes; failure to meet deadlines changes ending state (e.g., Jennifer's survival)
Time-gated: Jennifer saved only if boreworm defeated within time limit; Evil One represents final confrontation
Sustain mechanic during exploration and combat
Post-Evil One defeat: mask reveals evil intent, setting up destruction objective

typical_relief_triggers: Clearing all monsters in a room (map appears with directional choice)
typical_tension_triggers: Encountering boss fights with survival stakes (Jennifer/David at risk)
Navigating non-linear maze with backtracking requirement
Managing Power Meter availability for transformations
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Tension escalates through time management pressure and narrative stakes; relief comes from clearing combat arenas and discovering safe items; non-linear structure creates sustained navigation tension punctuated by combat relief cycles

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Health and weapon pickups scattered throughout mansion; optional exploration in non-linear rooms

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to basic combat, weapon variety, and non-linear exploration within moderate time pressure
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating enemy encounters; first major time-gated boss (Boreworm, Level 2); power meter mechanic deepens with transformation utility
difficulty_curve.late_game: Final confrontation with Evil One; mask's true intentions revealed; destruction objective replaces survival objective
notable_spikes: Level 2 boreworm encounter: Jennifer's life contingent on time limit completion
Evil One fight: David's rescue becomes primary objective; difficulty spike from narrative stakes and enemy complexity
Mask destruction phase: asymmetrical challenge after main boss defeat

notable_level_patterns.description: Players navigate interconnected mansion rooms with non-linear layout. Map provided after clearing monsters in each room. Time limits on each level create branching narrative outcomes based on speed/completion.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 1 (introductory mansion exploration); Level 2 (Jennifer rescue under time pressure)

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels feature boss fights tied to character rescue objectives. Success within time limit preserves character survival; failure alters plot state permanently.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 2 (Boreworm - Jennifer); later levels (Evil One - David)

notable_level_patterns.description: Access to powered-up form (Terror Mask fusion) gated by Eldritch Orb collection. Resource scarcity in levels creates strategic transformation timing decisions.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All levels (Power Meter mechanic scales with enemy density and boss difficulty)

notable_level_patterns.description: Four distinct endings determined by character survival states (both alive, Jennifer only, David only, neither). Time pressure directly feeds ending variation.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Meta-pattern across all 6 levels; culminates in final confrontation and mask destruction
